title: Shift-aware pseudo-labeling on only the easiest test rows
summary: Recent tabular self-training and shift-aware self-training papers report gains when pseudo-labels are both high-confidence and distribution-consistent.
rationale: Recent tabular self-training and shift-aware self-training papers report gains when pseudo-labels are both high-confidence and distribution-consistent. With a large unlabeled test set and known train-test shift, extremely selective pseudo-labeling could help more than generic semisupervision.
implementation_hint: Run the current best ensemble, keep only the most extreme-confidence test rows after density or adversarial-shift filtering, and retrain once with small weights. Prefer curriculum entry, temporal/seed consistency, and agreement across XGB/CatBoost; abort if forward-fold simulation degrades.
expected_effect: Could slightly adapt the model toward the test distribution and improve rare-pattern ranking without changing the core pipeline.
risk: High leakage risk in evaluation design and easy to fool yourself with transductive overfitting; only worth trying with strict simulated pseudo-label CV and tiny weights.
